title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Illinois Tool Works, Canadian Pacific And Deere
text: On 12/28/23, Illinois Tool Works, Canadian Pacific Kansas City, and Deere will all trade ex-dividend for their respective upcoming dividends. Illinois Tool Works, will pay its quarterly dividend of $1.40 on 1/11/24, Canadian Pacific Kansas City will pay its quarterly dividend of $0.19 on 1/29/24, and Deere will pay its quarterly dividend of $1.47 on 2/8/24.

title: Our Top 10 Dividend Growth Stocks - December 2023
text: We focus on selecting high-growth dividend stocks that have rapidly grown their dividends in the recent past rather than a high current yield. We use our proprietary models to rate quantitatively and qualitatively and select the top ten names from an initial list of nearly 400 dividend stocks. We provide criteria for selecting high-growth dividend stocks and offer a step-by-step guide on structuring a portfolio based on this strategy.

title: Deere & Company Raises Quarterly Dividend
text: MOLINE, Ill. , Dec. 6, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The Deere & Company (NYSE: DE) Board of Directors today declared a quarterly dividend of $1.47 per share payable February 8, 2024, to stockholders of record on December 29, 2023.

title: 3 Undervalued Agriculture Stocks to Harvest Long-Term Gains
text: Agriculture stocks have been experiencing a downturn due to declining crop prices and high costs associated with interest rates. This may offer an opportunity to get into some undervalued agriculture stocks looking to gain from precision agriculture.

title: John Deere Delivers Enhanced Customer Solution for Self-Repair
text: MOLINE, Ill. , Dec. 4, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- John Deere (NYSE: DE) announced today an enhanced self-repair solution, available initially in the U.S. through its Equipment Mobile app, enabling customers to remotely download secure software updates directly to embedded controllers on compatible 4G-connected John Deere equipment.

title: 5 Relatively Secure And Cheap Dividend Stocks To Invest In - December 2023
text: This article is part of our monthly series where we highlight five large-cap, relatively safe, dividend-paying companies offering large discounts to their historical norms. We go over our filtering process to select just five conservative DGI stocks from more than 7,500 companies that are traded on U.S. exchanges, including OTC networks. In addition to the primary list that yields just over 3%, we present two other groups of five DGI stocks each, with the goal of moderate to high yields.
